texto: Sometimes the hardest goodbyes are the ones never said, the ones that always just hang there in the back of the mind like a dark cloud. There's so much to say but no one to say it to because the person you want most to hear it is already gone. That's how he felt. Sorrow, regret, a wound so deep it didn't even bleed. Like a puncture wound, an ache that didn't heal but just hurt. He didn't know if he wanted it to heal. That'd be too much like a final goodbye.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Virginia Brown: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Sometimes the hardest goodbyes are the ones never said, the ones that always just hang there in t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death, goodbyes pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.

frase_original: Sometimes the hardest goodbyes are the ones never said, the ones that always just hang there in the back of the mind like a dark cloud. There's so much to say but no one to say it to because the person you want most to hear it is already gone. That's how he felt. Sorrow, regret, a wound so deep it didn't even bleed. Like a puncture wound, an ache that didn't heal but just hurt. He didn't know if he wanted it to heal. That'd be too much like a final goodbye.

texto: It was all he'd felt for too long to change now. Maybe it was too late for any other kind of life. This was all he knew. It was safe, insulated. Familiar. An absense of emotion kept him sane. Or what passed for it.
explicacao: Citação de Virginia Brown sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “It was all he'd felt for too long to change now. Maybe it was too late for any other kind of life…” articula reflexao, loss-of-hope, reflection em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e luto.
